scope: Algebra I foundation lesson content correctness
findings: The three linear-equation forms are introduced correctly: slope-intercept, point-slope, and standard form.
The point-slope worked example with slope 4 through (3, -2) correctly becomes y + 2 = 4(x - 3), preserving the inner negative sign.
Pause problem 1 correctly converts y - 1 = 3(x - 2) into y = 3x - 5.
The two-point example through (-1, 2) and (3, -6) correctly computes slope -2 and simplifies to y = -2x.
The standard-form conversion from y = (2/3)x + 4 correctly clears fractions and flips signs to produce 2x - 3y = -12.
Pause problem 2 correctly computes slope 4 through (1, -3) and (3, 5), then simplifies to y = 4x - 7.
release_notes: Foundation math only; no AP, College Board, Common App, CEEB, accreditation, NCAA, or college-admissions claims.
